title: United States restricts access to the most advanced artificial intelligence models from OpenAI and Anthropic
summary: The U.S. government authorizes the use of Anthropic's Mythos 5 for trusted organizations, while limiting the launch of OpenAI's GPT-5.6 Sol, generating an intense debate about state control of AI.

title: The rise in prices of RAM and SSDs transforms the semiconductor market
summary: The increase in chip prices leads manufacturers to use recycled components and new players like CXMT to challenge industry leaders, while consumers seek alternatives.

title: Apple will skip the Pro and Max versions of the M6 chip and launch an M7 focused on artificial intelligence
summary: According to reports, the company will only launch a basic M6 chip this year and will focus on the M7 generation with advanced AI capabilities, planned for 2027.

title: Intel Arc B770 graphics cards emerge as an alternative to NVIDIA and AMD
summary: Rumors and initial analysis indicate that Intel's Arc GPUs could offer competitive performance in the gaming graphics market.

title: PS4 emulator ShadPS4 allows online play without PSN and emulates Bloodborne on PC
summary: The emulator update adds multiplayer functions and compatibility with the popular FromSoftware title, expanding the catalog of playable games on PC.

title: YouTube removes the 'dislike' button on Shorts and adds 2x playback and clean mode
summary: The platform renews its short video section with new visual and speed features, while removing the dislike button.

title: Microsoft extends Windows 10 security updates until October 2027
summary: The company delays the end of free support for Windows 10, giving users more time to upgrade to Windows 11.

title: More than a hundred lawsuits shake artificial intelligence companies over copyright
summary: Publishers and content creators take OpenAI, Microsoft and other companies to court for unauthorized use of their works to train AI models.

title: Valve's Steam Machines generate excitement: reservations worth thousands of dollars and an improved clone
summary: The return of Valve's console-PC format triggers speculation on eBay, while a clone called STIM Machine offers superior hardware at a lower price.

title: AMD Ryzen 7 5800X3D arrives in Spain at a good price and sells out in international offers
summary: The processor with 3D V-Cache is available in the Spanish market, while in the United States it is recommended to opt for more affordable DDR5 CPUs.

title: Europe launches its own AI chatbot Eustella and asserts its digital sovereignty versus the U.S.
summary: The European Union presents its own artificial intelligence assistant and speaks out to develop technologies independent of the big US tech companies.

title: Tesla settles lawsuit over accident with its FSD autonomous driving system
summary: The out-of-court settlement closes the lawsuit while federal investigations into the system's safety continue.

title: FBI warns that Russian hackers attack Signal backup keys
summary: Security agencies warn about a spying campaign aimed at stealing recovery keys from the encrypted messaging app.

title: Russian hackers responsible for the $2.5 billion cyberattack on Jaguar Land Rover
summary: A report links Russian cybercriminal groups to the largest data theft in the history of the British car manufacturer.

title: IBM presents the first sub-1 nanometer chip, the smallest and most powerful in the world
summary: The new manufacturing process promises to revolutionize the semiconductor industry with greater energy efficiency and performance.

title: Onsemi acquires Synaptics for $7 billion in a stock swap
summary: The merger will combine edge artificial intelligence with power chips to create a new semiconductor giant.

title: OpenAI hires Uber India director to lead its Asia expansion
summary: The artificial intelligence company bets on an experienced executive to boost its presence in the largest market outside the United States.

title: TikTok moves towards becoming a super app with financial services and commerce
summary: The social network continues adding features to retain users and diversify its revenue streams beyond advertising.

title: OpenAI, SpaceX and other big tech companies develop their own chips to reduce dependence on NVIDIA
summary: The semiconductor shortage and the rise of artificial intelligence drive companies to design custom hardware, increasing competition with traditional manufacturers.

title: Startup Lindy abandons Anthropic's Claude model for DeepSeek to save millions
summary: The high cost of Anthropic's APIs leads the AI company to migrate to the Chinese model, drastically reducing its operating expenses.
